mnemonics: Natural Disasters: H.E.A.T. (Hurricanes, Earthquakes, Avalanches, Tsunamis)
Think of the '3 Rs' for Natural Resource Conservation: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle.
Remember the main Greenhouse Gases as C.O.W.S. (Carbon dioxide, Ozone, Water vapor, methaneS)
keyPoints: Natural resources are classified as renewable (e.g., solar, wind) and non-renewable (e.g., fossil fuels, minerals).
Human activities like deforestation and pollution accelerate natural resource depletion and environmental degradation.
Sustainable management of natural resources is crucial for balancing current needs with the ability of future generations to meet their own.
commonMistakes: Confusing the greenhouse effect with ozone layer depletion; they are distinct environmental issues.
Assuming all natural resources are infinite; non-renewable resources are finite and can be exhausted.
Overlooking the interconnectedness of natural systems; an action in one area (e.g., dumping e-waste) can negatively impact a seemingly separate natural system (e.g., water pollution).
